description: Wire up directory presence data into time estimation for more accurate tracking

userStories.title: estimate_time_v2 accepts presence intervals
userStories.description: Add a `presence_intervals: &[TimeInterval]` parameter to `estimate_time_v2`. Presence intervals are clipped to the query window and passed in alongside AI sessions. When git events are absent for a repo but presence data exists, presence intervals alone contribute to estimated time.
acceptanceCriteria: Function signature changes to: estimate_time_v2(events, ai_sessions, presence_intervals, session_gap_minutes, first_commit_minutes)
All existing callers updated to pass &[] for presence_intervals
All existing estimate_time_v2 unit tests still pass
New unit test: presence intervals with no git events → time = sum of presence durations
New unit test: presence intervals merge with git session intervals correctly

userStories.title: Presence intervals anchor git session starts
userStories.description: When a presence interval overlaps the tentative credit window of a git session (first_event - credit), the git session start is pulled back to the presence interval start instead of applying the estimated credit. This yields a more accurate session start time.
acceptanceCriteria: If presence interval starts before (first_event - credit), git session start = presence.start
If presence interval starts after first_event, no anchoring (current credit logic applies)
If no presence overlaps the credit window, behavior is identical to pre-change
Unit test: presence started 45 min before first commit (credit=30) → session start = presence.start
Unit test: presence started after first commit → no anchoring, credit logic unchanged
Unit test: presence interval covers only part of credit window → session start = presence.start (presence wins over credit)

userStories.title: query_activity fetches and passes presence per repo
userStories.description: In `query_activity`, call `query_presence` for the time window, look up each repo's presence intervals, and pass them into `estimate_time_v2`. Presence intervals must be clipped to [from, to] before passing (query_presence already clips them).
acceptanceCriteria: query_activity calls query_presence once per query (not per repo)
Each repo's presence intervals are looked up by repo_path from the BTreeMap returned by query_presence
Repos with no presence data pass &[] (existing behavior preserved)
Integration test: repo with presence data + commits → estimated_time increases vs no-presence baseline
Integration test: repo with presence only (no commits) still does NOT create a RepoSummary entry (existing test query_activity_presence_does_not_create_repos must still pass)

userStories.title: global_estimated_time passes presence to estimate_time_v2
userStories.description: Update `global_estimated_time` to also accept the per-repo presence intervals and forward them to `estimate_time_v2`. Since global_estimated_time works on already-built RepoSummary slices, presence intervals must be stored on RepoSummary or passed separately.
acceptanceCriteria: RepoSummary gains a `presence_intervals: Vec<TimeInterval>` field
query_activity populates presence_intervals on each RepoSummary
global_estimated_time reads repo.presence_intervals and passes them to estimate_time_v2
All existing RepoSummary constructions in tests updated (compiler-guided)
Unit test for global_estimated_time with overlapping presence across two repos: no double-counting

userStories.title: Update doc comment on estimate_time_v2
userStories.description: The existing doc comment says '/// No presence data.' — update it to document the new presence_intervals parameter, its role in anchoring session starts, and the merge semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Doc comment updated to describe presence_intervals parameter
Doc comment describes anchoring logic (presence start < credit window start → use presence start)
Doc comment notes that presence intervals are unioned with git + AI intervals before final merge
cargo doc builds without warnings on query.rs

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
